title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for tls, sse-s3, sse-kms, bucket keys, and encryption boundaries and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for encryption and key-authorization contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 6701 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Key Rotation Revocation Drill.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Key Rotation Revocation Drill, keep Key Rotation Revocation Drill connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
